message: Finally, a decent request I’ve been waiting for. Ugh.
Really. I want to be happy about it, but isn't Meryl kind of annoying?
Hey, it's rude to say that about the client who gave us this job.
But, no matter how famous she is, that attitude is something I can't tolerate.
If you feel that way, should we cancel the request?
Cancel!? D-Don’t say such foolish things!
Work is work; let's push through without letting personal feelings interfere.
In the quiet room, only the sound of Yuriko tapping on the keys can be heard.
I hope I can pull the data by accessing the database, but if that's not possible, there's nothing I can do but give up.
A tense mood is hanging in the room, probably because of the first big job.
This isn't the time to be doing this.
I'm tapping the keys while looking at the monitor.
Yuriko, with a serious expression like never before, looks splendid.
If the requests keep coming in, this kind of daily routine will probably continue.
It's probably because there are job requests, her eyes shining and looking happy.
she can't hide the excitement of this first fulfilling feeling.
I'm busy right now, please don't talk to me.
I'm sorry, Boss. Please don't talk to me right now.
Finally, the chance to prove myself as a detective has come!
Hey, I wonder if everything's okay.
You seem quite carefree, don't you?
The real question is what to do from here.
Yeah, why did she decide to look for her daughter now after all this time?
I’m sorry, Boss. Please don’t talk to me right now.
Meryl is our client, so no matter how many thanks we express, it won’t be enough.
You shouldn't say it's troublesome. It's just a bit challenging.
Please stop pretending it’s nothing and sighing.
I thought maybe Meryl isn’t so bad after all, even though I’ve struggled with her.
Well, it can't be helped, can it? I’ve never really liked that type.
Right now isn’t the time to be talking about that.
But don’t you think it’s strange to look for her daughter after 16 years?
Wasn't there some kind of situation?
Oh, I made a mistake! Boss, please be quiet.
I'm busy right now, so please don't talk to me.
Ah! I pressed the wrong key. Ugh!
I'm sorry, Boss. Please don't talk to me right now.
Anyway, I'll contact the midwifery association in New York State.
Oh, contacting the database directly might be faster.
I might just do it myself.
If you think so, then go for it!
So, I want to sort out the details of the request.
Yeah, right. The request is to find Meryl's daughter, but we should first look for someone who knows her foster parents.
In this case, the midwife named Anna fits that description. But if that Anna is a fraud, then there's no way to find her.
Because she was a properly registered person, I could safely give birth.
Boss? It seems like there's a misunderstanding, but I wasn't listening to Meryl at all.
Oh, is that so? Your face changed so much regardless.
If I can find Anna, I’ll know where Meryl's daughter is.
Ah, I found it! Boss, I got it!
Well, the person is named Anna Skakki, and she lives in Little Italy.
Little Italy? That's just around the corner.
When you say registered, you mean with the midwife association, right?
Don't you think it's rare to have midwives these days?
It's been 16 years, so it's not really that rare. Plus, there are still plenty of people who can't give birth in a hospital.
Anyway, it seems we can't talk if we don't find Anna.
If we find out Anna's contact info, we should go see her and chat, right?
Yeah, that's right. Other than that, there's not much we can do.
That’s rude. I'm investigating here, aren't I? The only one with nothing to do is the Boss.
Even if you talk to me, I won’t answer. It’s not a good time.
What’s the quickest way to carry out the request?
Well, we have no choice but to find the only lead, midwife Anna.
The only other clue is a single photo.
Just by looking at the photo,
Ah, this is so-and-so from somewhere!
I wish I could recognize them like that.
That would have been nice and easy.
I can’t waste time, you know.
It's surprising that Meryl had a daughter, but for a woman her age, it's not so uncommon.
Still, it must have been a hat to hide her face, but didn't it stand out even more?
Meryl had a pained expression, but why did she decide to look for her daughter now?
She had been successful for quite a while, so if she put her mind to it, she could have searched anytime.
If she really intended to search, shouldn't she have started looking much earlier?
But I guess it's not good to be suspicious like that.
To clear this request safely, the first step is to find a midwife named Anna.
Once we know Anna's whereabouts, we can get information from her for the next step.
Yuriko is investigating Anna. But I wonder if she remembers something from 16 years ago?
Maybe I shouldn't have taken this request.
